pastorsIntroText:
The partner pastors of Prison Revival, on a weekly rotation basis, conduct the "Guided Prayer" which can be easily done behind bars, and the "Sunday Service Order and Sermon Files" to help inmates worship in their rooms.
They also answer "faith-related letters" from inmates, and lead the "Saturday Prayer Meeting" (conducted via Zoom).
pastorChoiOverlayText1: With the wide and deep heart of Jesus Christ, Prison Revival delivers the Gospel and love to souls behind bars.
pastorChoiOverlayText2: Prison Revival does not judge those behind bars as 'those whose flaws are exposed.' We spread the Gospel with the heart of the Lord, who loved them to the end (John 13:1).
pastorChoiOverlayText3: If you share love and the Gospel with Prison Revival, they will meet Jesus personally. And they will draw their life plan again and live a new life.
pastorChoiOverlaySign: Prison Revival & Angel Tree / Associate Pastor Joonmoon Choi
pastorBonghoOverlayText1: "The people living in darkness have seen a great light; on those living in the land of the shadow of death a light has dawned." Matthew 4:16
pastorBonghoOverlayText2: I hope that the light that the Lord has shone upon me will now become a window that shines upon others.
pastorBonghoOverlaySign: Prison Revival & Angel Tree / Associate Pastor Bongho Choi
pastorHeewonOverlayText1: If you want to experience the Lord who is still alive and active,
pastorHeewonOverlayText2: If you want to see up close that the power of the Gospel can still transform souls,
pastorHeewonOverlayText3: Please join us in this Prison Revival ministry to taste and enjoy the Lord's amazing grace.
pastorHeewonOverlaySign: Prison Revival & Angel Tree / Associate Pastor Heewon Kim
pastorSeungroOverlayText1: Jesus said that caring for those in the lowest and most marginalized places is caring for 'Me'.
pastorSeungroOverlayText2: Beyond simply helping the souls behind bars, we invite you to a place of grace to meet Jesus, who is weeping with the groaning souls there.
pastorSeungroOverlaySign: Prison Revival & Angel Tree / Associate Pastor Seungro Lee
pastorHyeonminOverlayText1: "For even the Son of Man did not come to be served, but to serve, and to give his life as a ransom for many."
pastorHyeonminOverlayText3: Jesus said that when He came to this earth, He did not come to call the righteous, but sinners. If we have received the grace of salvation freely as sinners, I am proud to say that there is no life more precious than a life that glorifies the Lord with blessed service in obedience to Jesus' words.
pastorHyeonminOverlayText4: We joyfully wait for you to be used as a channel to flow the love you received from the Lord to another soul.
pastorHyeonminOverlaySign: Prison Revival & Angel Tree / Associate Pastor Hyeonmin Choi
pastorGeumjuOverlayText1: This is the great commission, like a will, of Jesus who died on the cross, taking upon Himself all our sins, pain, and shame.
pastorGeumjuOverlayText2: 'Go and make disciples of all nations, preaching the Gospel.'
pastorGeumjuOverlayText3: Behind the walls, countless souls with 'poor spirits' are waiting to hear the Gospel and accept Jesus as their Savior.
pastorGeumjuOverlayText4: If there is anyone wondering how to repay the love and grace of God our Father who made us His children, please join the literature mission of Prison Revival.
pastorGeumjuOverlayText5: You will experience the joy of spreading the Gospel.
pastorGeumjuOverlayText6: Please willingly accept the invitation to the heavenly banquet that you can enjoy in life.
pastorGeumjuOverlaySign: Prison Revival & Angel Tree / Ministry Partner Geumju Lim
navSubMannaAngel: Manna Bridge in Angel Tree Ministry
navSubPrisonAngelGuide: Prison Angel Tree Ministry & Support Guide
mannaSeumText:
There are children left home alone due to the sudden incarceration of their parents.

They are minor-headed households who, at an age when they should still be protected in their parents' arms, must cook for themselves, attend school, and worry about living expenses and their future. Although they pretend to be fine on the outside, loneliness, anxiety, and pain that cannot be easily shared with anyone reside in their hearts.

The Manna Bridge in Seum project is a ministry that delivers monthly growth support funds to these children so they do not give up on their dreams or lose hope due to economic difficulties, until their parents are released and return home.

By supporting each child with 80,000 to 100,000 KRW monthly, we help them buy necessary school supplies, have meals, and continue a normal daily life where they can hang out with friends. The amount itself may not be large, but to the children, it becomes proof of love that "someone remembers me" and gives them hope to dream of the future again.

We are not just delivering pocket money, but helping God's love and comfort flow into the lives of these wounded children. Your small sponsorship can protect a child's today and become a seed that changes their tomorrow.
mannaSeumHowText:
You can apply for regular sponsorship (automatic transfer) through the "Manna Bridge in Seum Regular Sponsorship" at the bottom, choosing any amount from 5,000 KRW to 300,000 KRW monthly.
Your regular sponsorship funds will be used as growth support for children living with difficulties in the absence of their parents, helping them grow healthy and prepare for their future.

For reference, tax-deductible receipts can be issued for Seum donations.
Changing a child's life does not start in a grand place. When someone's small interest and love come together, children gain the strength to live with hope again. We ask for your warm companionship.
mannaSeumBtn: Regular Sponsorship for Angel Tree Manna Bridge in Seum
mannaSeumDesc: A sisterhood ministry that provides continuous support for minors living in single-person households. Through regular sponsorship, we support the actual lives of children while serving as a conduit for God's love and the Gospel.
mannaAngelTitle: Manna Bridge in Angel Tree Project
mannaAngelText:
There are children left all alone in the world due to the sudden incarceration of their parents.

At an age when they should still be protected in their parents' arms, they are getting by day by day, eating meals alone without a guardian, going to and from school, and worrying about living expenses. Although they look normal on the outside, loneliness, anxiety, and wounds that cannot be easily shared with anyone reside in their hearts.

The Manna Bridge in Angel Tree project is a ministry that delivers love and hope to these minor-headed households by supporting them with necessary gifts, daily necessities, school supplies, and household items every month.

We are not just sending things, but wanting to convey the heart that "you are not alone" and "someone loves and supports you" to the children. A small gift becomes a great comfort to them and gives them the courage and hope to live again.

Your sponsorship filled with love cannot replace their parents' empty space, but it can become a conduit for God's love that warmly embraces that empty space.
mannaAngelHowText:
You can apply for regular sponsorship (automatic transfer) through the "Manna Bridge in Angel Tree Regular Sponsorship" at the bottom, choosing the amount you wish monthly.

Sponsorship accounts are prepared in two ways.

● SAM International Account:
SAM International / Industrial Bank of Korea (IBK) / 073-050065-01-069 / Tax-deductible receipt available

● Prison Revival & Angel Tree Account:
Prison Revival Angel Tree / Woori Bank / 1005-704-852271 / Tax-deductible receipt unavailable

We would appreciate it if you could join us by selecting an account that suits your situation.

The love that protects a child's today can change their tomorrow and become a hope that builds their future. We ask for your warm companionship.
mannaAngelDesc: A sisterhood ministry that provides continuous support for minors living in single-person households. Through regular sponsorship, we support the actual lives of children while serving as a conduit for God's love and the Gospel.
prisonAngelGuideDesc: Content for Prison Angel Tree Ministry & Support Guide is being prepared.
progBookTitle: Book Ministry Delivering the Gospel Behind Walls
progBookDesc:
Prison Revival sends hundreds of Bibles and Christian books to inmates every month.

Many inmates did not have much opportunity to encounter churches or the Bible before their incarceration. Therefore, we provide the 'Duranno Woori Bible,' which even beginners can easily read and understand.

Furthermore, to ensure that those who encounter the gospel for the first time can read without burden, we also support books written by missionary Eun-Mi Lim, who has led many souls to the Lord.

Receiving books inside prisons requires a complex process of registering incoming books with the Ministry of Justice. However, through the dedicated service of our book dispatch team, we register and send each book with care, creating a conduit for the gospel.

Under God's grace, the necessary monthly finances are being filled, and hundreds of Bibles and Christian books are delivered to correctional institutions nationwide. Through these books, we continue to witness amazing things: souls in despair encountering the Word, meeting Jesus Christ personally, and discovering new hope in life.

A single Bible or Christian book can be a seed that changes someone's life and transforms eternity.

We would be grateful if you could pray for this precious ministry and partner with us in support and love.
progBookQuote:
“Your word is a lamp for my feet, a light on my path.” (Psalm 119:105)
Today, that light is reaching even behind the walls.
progBookBtnBible: Sponsor Bible: Large Print Korean-English Vision Bible
progBookBtnBook1: Sponsor Missionary Book: From the Lord 1 (Daily Warm Words from God)
progBookBtnBook2: Sponsor Missionary Book: From the Lord 2 (Words of the Lord reviving my soul)
progBookBtnBook3: Sponsor Missionary Book: Is It You, the One God Seeks?
progJesusDesc:
Life behind bars is restricted in many ways. That is why one of the most frequent requests from those who meet Jesus in correctional facilities is a drawing of Jesus.

Those who read the Word, pray, and begin their life of faith want to keep an image of Jesus they can look at and meditate on. However, behind the walls, even such a small hope is often difficult to realize.

Thankfully, through President Janet Hyun's 'JESUS AGAIN' project, that hope has become a reality.

At Prison Revival, there is a New Letter Team that prints out the first letter in color and sends it to those who apply for meditation. Through their service, a postcard of Jesus' face containing the decision prayer is delivered to the inmates who apply for meditation for the first time.

This one small postcard becomes the first Gospel for some, the beginning of faith to return to Jesus for others, and a gift of hope to endure difficult times for yet others.

The JESUS AGAIN project is underway to spread the Gospel beyond Korea to the whole world, and the ministry is expanding so that more people can meet Jesus and experience His love.

We hope that a single drawing will become a channel to preach the Gospel to a soul, and a single prayer will become a seed to change a person's life.

We would be grateful if you could pray for and support this precious ministry so that it can continue to spread the light of the Gospel behind the walls and all over the world.
progJesusQuote: “A small drawing that leads back to Jesus, and God's love for a single soul.”
progJesusFooter: The JESUS AGAIN Project continues to preach the Gospel today.
navSubChatRoom: Join Prison Revival Main Group Chat
heroDesc: Sharing the gospel with inmates and walking together toward renewal.
introPageDesc: Prison Revival, inviting you to the journey of faith.
introMainText:
Prison Revival is a lay gospel organization dedicated to sharing the gospel with approximately 64,000 inmates in 57 correctional facilities nationwide.

Based on Missionary Eunmi Lim's daily meditations, we produce various Christian content and faith-based materials for inmates.

Volunteers send these materials weekly through the internet post office.

Through these letters, we help inmates meditate on God's word and experience life transformation.
recommendationText: I believe the Lord is with Prison Revival in every ministry. I bless everyone who joins us!
ceoGreetingText:
Hello, I am Oh Hyuk, the CEO of Prison Revival & Angel Tree.
Our goal is to bring gospel and change to 64,000 inmates across the country.
We believe a single letter can change a life.
Thank you for your support.
advisorGreetingText:
Just as Jonah was sent to save Nineveh, the wicked city of Assyria,
I believe God established and sent "Prison Revival"!
Imitating Jesus who followed God's will and love,
let us become volunteers praying so that our brothers and sisters behind bars may receive more blessings,
sharing the Gospel with even one more person to spread God's love,
and blessing our brothers and sisters behind bars to know God's love in Jesus' name!
Amen 🙏
pressLawToday: [Interview] Prison Revival: Delivering Hope Beyond Prejudice in Prison Ministry
pressGoodNews: Letters Sent Beyond Prison Walls, Awakening Souls

volProg2Desc: Pray daily for a specifically assigned inmate.
volProg3Desc: The Prison Revival Homeless Ministry goes beyond providing meals and daily necessities to the homeless, serving to help restore their lives in the Gospel. Through regular meetings, we build trust and relationships, delivering love and comfort to those in loneliness and pain. We also share strength and hope to rise again through continuous care and spiritual support. Believing that one person's change leads to another's recovery, we dream of a Kingdom of God ministry where lives are transformed by the Gospel and communities are built. We hope to be a channel of His love where it is needed most.
volProg4Desc: Guiding inmates through their Bible study journey.
angelDescMain: Please join us in the Angel Tree ministry to help young children on behalf of incarcerated parents.
angelIntroText:
Angel Tree is a ministry that shares love and the Gospel with the children of incarcerated parents on their behalf.

We deliver gifts to children facing emotional and economic hardships due to their parents' absence, aiming to send a message that goes beyond simple support: "You are not forgotten."

Centered around Christmas, Angel Tree plays a vital role in restoring family connections through gifts and letters delivered in the parents' names.

Currently, we are also conducting the 'Angel Tree Manna Bridge' ministry, which provides continuous support for minors living in single-person households.

It is an expanded ministry designed to support the actual lives of children through regular sponsorship while simultaneously sharing the Gospel.

In this way, Angel Tree is becoming a conduit for the Gospel, moving beyond one-time gifts to continuously restore the lives of children and their families.
angelChristmasText: Prison Revival & Angel Tree is a Christmas gift project that delivers gifts along with the Lord's Gospel to children of inmates on behalf of parents who cannot give gifts to their children in prison during Christmas.
angelChristmasHowText: Guardian or self (5-19 years old) applies through the Angel Tree Kakao Channel -> Confirmed through parent's current incarceration status and family relation certificate -> Gift application through the Angel Tree survey link -> Receive Christmas Angel Tree gift by courier

volGuideDescMain: How to join as a Prison Volunteer.
volGuideIntroText:
Prison Revival volunteers are lay ministers who serve as practical conduits for sharing the Gospel with those behind bars.

Participants join the ministry by printing and sending weekly meditation materials via the online post office, paying for the postage costs directly.

Rather than special qualifications or expertise, a sense of responsibility and dedication to consistently delivering the Word is required. While anyone can learn and participate, continuous commitment is the most important aspect of this ministry.

A single letter serves as a channel to transform a person's life, and the participation of one volunteer connects directly to one soul.

Currently, 480 volunteers are serving in this ministry, and we are praying for 3,000 volunteers to join us.
volGuideVideoDesc: Click on the video to see the detailed guide.
